name: RegistryEvent (Value Set)
definition: Sysmon Event ID 13 records registry value modifications.
trigger_scenarios: Sysmon emits this event when a configured RegistryEvent rule observes a registry value being set. Microsoft documents that the event records the written value for DWORD and QWORD registry value types.

key_fields.explanation: The registry value path uses Sysmon's abbreviated root names such as HKLM and HKU. Autorun, Office security, and Defender policy locations are high-value targets for persistence and defense evasion.

Contains the value written for DWORD and QWORD registry value types according to Microsoft. A written path under C:\\Users\\, C:\\ProgramData\\, or C:\\Windows\\Temp can reveal a user-writable payload location.
Identifies the process that set the value and provides a stable join key to Sysmon Event ID 1 for command-line context.

false_positives: Approved administration, management agents, and deployment tools can generate this telemetry.
Baseline expected hosts, signed binaries, and change windows before suppression.
detection_notes: Alert when TargetObject is HKCU\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run or HKLM\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run and Details points to C:\\Users\\, C:\\ProgramData\\, C:\\Windows\\Temp, or another user-writable path. Microsoft documents Event 13 as registry value modification telemetry and notes that DWORD/QWORD written values are captured, so the TargetObject plus Details pair can directly show an autorun payload or a defense-evasion value change. This supports T1112 Modify Registry; correlate ProcessGuid to Event 1 and verify whether the Image is an approved installer or management agent.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 13
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, ProcessGuid
spl_snippet: index=sysmon EventCode=13 | table _time, host, Image, ProcessGuid
sample_log:
UtcTime: 2026-07-13 01:00:00.000
EventType: SetValue
TargetObject: HKCU\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\Updater
Details: C:\\ProgramData\\updater.exe
Image: C:\\Users\\Public\\updater.exe
ProcessGuid: {REDACTED}

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 13
queries.query:
Event
| where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 13
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is collected into Event.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields described on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 13
queries.query:
index=sysmon source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=13
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Apply the event-specific field conditions after validating local field extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 13
assumptions: Confirm Provider is Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon because event IDs are not globally unique.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Sysmon Event ID 13
queries.query:
title: Sysmon Event ID 13
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: sysmon
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 13
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the documented field conditions and correlations in the destination SIEM.

value_references.meaning: A process modified a registry value.
value_references.security_relevance: Use EventType together with the other documented event fields and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: Sysmon records the written data for DWORD and QWORD registry value types.
value_references.security_relevance: Use Details together with the other documented event fields and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Sysmon Event ID 13 mean?
faqs.answer: A process set or modified a registry value.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 13 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Normal applications and management agents make frequent registry changes.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 13 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Evaluate TargetObject and Details, then join ProcessGuid to Event 1 to identify the responsible command line.
